prompt: My team wants to expand into the European market. We're a US-based SaaS, $5M ARR, 22 employees. The plan: hire 2 EU-based AEs in Q2, localize the product (3 languages), get GDPR-compliant, set up an EU data center, launch a .eu site, and start outbound into UK/Germany/France by Q3. Total budget ask: $800k over 12 months. I have 18 months of runway. Please do an adversarial review including whether this plan makes sense as structured.

assertions.text: The report identifies this as a mixed or partially one-way door decision (hiring is reversible; GDPR/data center commitments are harder to undo)
The report includes a 'Think Big, Work Small' section that recommends sequencing the plan to validate assumptions before committing full budget
The report flags the risk of running GDPR compliance, data center setup, hiring, localization, and outbound all in parallel — recommending phased approach
The report flags the runway risk: $800k spend on 18 months runway is a significant portion, and EU expansion often takes longer than planned
The report recommends validating EU demand before building infrastructure (e.g., test with existing inbound, close 2-3 EU deals manually before investing in localization)
